title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
At the Curlew Hotel, a man named Dick arrives and claims he is staying there because his apartment requires renovation and his doctor suggested the seaside air. He reveals he specifically requested room 413 and informs Colin Lamb that he has called the county police commissioner and is waiting for Hercule Poirot.

When Poirot arrives, the group sits together. Dick attempts to discuss the investigation, but Poirot interrupts, claiming his own London apartment also requires renovation. Poirot suggests that Colin brought him the case not to solve it, but to challenge and mock him with a problem that cannot be solved simply by sitting in an armchair.

Poirot claims to know the identities of the killers responsible for the man at 19 Wilbraham Crescent and the death of Ingrid Bret, though he admits he lacks the evidence to prove it. He explains that once the correct method is found, the facts will fall into place. Using a metaphor, Poirot describes a "dog" with extraordinary abilities—a source of information possessing an extraordinary memory that can repeat specific, accurate details of conversations and events as if present at the scene.

The men discuss the inconsistencies of the case and the inaccuracy of newspaper reports. They review the facts regarding the death at 19 Wilbraham Crescent: a middle-aged man was found dead carrying a business card for an R.H. Curry of a non-existent insurance company. The investigation noted that a stenographer, Miss Hillar Webber, was requested by a Miss Mylees Pemmerton to go to the residence at three o'clock, at which time she discovered the body.
